title: pimenta filipes
side_effects: Pimenta filipes can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems due to the presence of oils and compounds that can be toxic in high concentrations. If ingested, pimenta filipes can lead to nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain, as well as interact with certain medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. In rare cases, excessive exposure to the plant's oils can cause headaches, dizziness, and even seizures, especially in individuals with pre-existing neurological conditions. Prolonged or repeated exposure to pimenta filipes can also lead to contact dermatitis, a type of skin inflammation that can be painful and itchy.
